title: Evangelion Wannabes
wikitext:
[]: Evangelion Wannabes?             Quote Post by bogey » Tue Mar 27, 2012 12:19 am whenever reading something on evangelion, it always states what an impact it had and how it inspired a slew of wannabe animes. can someone name some of these? I know about rahxephon but what else is there?

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI_01 » Tue Mar 27, 2012 12:25 am Maybe Escaflowne? I am not sure since it has been a long time since I have watched any of it and I did not see much of it at all. Eureka 7 too? I never got see all of it, but what I did see kinda had an NGE feel to it (at least to me it did)

[]:             Quote Post by Elk » Tue Mar 27, 2012 12:38 am MAGI_01 wrote: Maybe Escaflowne? I am not sure since it has been a long time since I have watched any of it and I did not see much of it at all. Eureka 7 too? I never got see all of it, but what I did see kinda had an NGE feel to it (at least to me it did)

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Mar 27, 2012 12:44 am The source material (Escaflowne manga) predates Eva. The obvious answer would be Brain Powered .

[]:             Quote Post by Dan The Cat » Tue Mar 27, 2012 3:02 am http://www.animenewsnetwork.co.uk/the-mike-toole-show/2011-06-05 all of these? :P personally i really liked rahxephon, the ending was amazing.

[]:             Quote Post by MAGI_01 » Tue Mar 27, 2012 4:00 am Elk wrote: Maybe a source that it draws on, but Escaflowne is definitely not a wannabe Evangelion.

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Tue Mar 27, 2012 2:29 pm I think Shuu Ouma from Guilty Crown is a bit of a Shinji clone from what I've heard of him.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Tue Mar 27, 2012 2:46 pm Off the top of my head, Shinji Ikari Raising Project.

[]:             Quote Post by Tankred » Tue Mar 27, 2012 2:52 pm Digimon Tamers.

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Tue Mar 27, 2012 3:07 pm chee wrote: Off the top of my head, Shinji Ikari Raising Project.

[]:             Quote Post by BiQ » Tue Mar 27, 2012 4:37 pm It's not really a wannabe in any sense of that word but something in Gasaraki hit me really hard with the "oh yeah, this was done soon after Eva all right", and not in a good way because honestly, I didn't bother to even try to get whatever it was trying to say to me, which didn't really seem like much. It looked very superficial to me. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Wed Mar 28, 2012 4:06 pm CJD wrote: Oh man, you have no idea. Shoe=Shinji Inori=Rei Haruka= Misato (That's Shoe's mother who's gone often, neglectful, works at the secret organization, and flirtatious) There's more but those were the most obvious for me.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Mar 29, 2012 1:18 am Ornette wrote: The source material (Escaflowne manga) predates Eva. The obvious answer would be Brain Powered .

[]:             Quote Post by CJD » Thu Mar 29, 2012 5:31 am driftking18594 wrote: I heard Guilty Crown is a Code Geass clone too. Come to think of it, their initials are the same, only reversed.

[]:             Quote Post by KnightmareX13 » Thu Mar 29, 2012 7:22 am CJD wrote: To be fair, it's not really a clone of anything. It's more accurate to call it a Frankenstein monster.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Mar 29, 2012 12:35 pm Bagheera wrote: I've been meaning to watch this for awhile. I'm only three episodes in, but . . . I'm not getting an NGE vibe from it. Like, at all.

[]:             Quote Post by Bagheera » Thu Mar 29, 2012 12:42 pm Ornette wrote: It's not, like at all. But in a conversation where "Evangelion wannabe" is involved, the obvious (as in it always comes up) is Brain Powered, but it's not a "clone", it's not really an attempt to copy, nor is it any attempt to replace, but it regularly gets used as a "this is Eva done right!". See: wiki

[]:             Quote Post by driftking18594 » Thu Mar 29, 2012 1:23 pm CJD wrote: To be fair, it's not really a clone of anything. It's more accurate to call it a Frankenstein monster.

[]: TAMERS! BD             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Fri Mar 30, 2012 11:16 pm Tankred wrote: Digimon Tamers.

[]:             Quote Post by bobbyfischer's ghost » Sat Mar 31, 2012 12:07 am Martian Successor Nadesico is one of the eva-style anime.
